risk_description: Distributed transaction inconsistency across Payment Service (Stripe/Wise) and internal Project/Ledger databases due to potential network failures or API timeouts during the Saga pattern execution.
root_cause: Reliance on distributed microservices and external payment gateways without native two-phase commit capability.
mitigation_strategy: Implement the Saga pattern with compensating transactions. Enforce strict idempotency on all financial webhooks. Implement automated nightly reconciliation jobs between Stripe/Wise ledgers and internal DB.
contingency_plan: Manual administrative override interface to force-update transaction states based on external gateway evidence.
monitoring_trigger: Detection of 'orphaned' transactions in PENDING state > 1 hour or webhook signature validation failures > 1%.

risk_description: AI hallucination or inaccuracy during SOW extraction and PII sanitization leading to incorrect project scoping or data leakage, compromising the vendor matching quality and compliance.
root_cause: Probabilistic nature of LLMs (OpenAI) and variability in unstructured SOW document formats.
mitigation_strategy: Implement 'Human-in-the-Loop' workflow requiring admin approval of extracted data. Use strict JSON schema validation (Zod) for LLM outputs. Configure confidence thresholds for automated processing.
contingency_plan: Fallback to manual data entry mode for SOWs that fail automated extraction thresholds.
monitoring_trigger: User rejection rate of AI-extracted fields > 15% or PII detection alerts in sanitized buckets.

risk_description: GDPR 'Right to Erasure' compliance failure due to complexity in selectively deleting PII from immutable audit logs and distributed microservices while retaining legally required financial records.
root_cause: Conflict between immutable audit trail requirements (SOC 2) and GDPR deletion rights in a distributed architecture.
mitigation_strategy: Design data schema with strict separation of PII and transactional data. Implement crypto-shredding (deleting the encryption key for specific user data) rather than physical row deletion for immutable logs.
contingency_plan: Engage legal counsel to define exact data retention waivers; manual database intervention scripts for urgent deletion requests.
monitoring_trigger: Failure of automated GDPR erasure test suite in CI/CD pipeline.

risk_description: Failure to meet p95 latency target of < 250ms due to overhead from inter-service communication (Gateway -> Service -> DB) and heavy vector search operations in PostgreSQL.
root_cause: Network hops in microservices architecture and compute-intensive pgvector HNSW index queries on large datasets.
mitigation_strategy: Implement Redis caching for high-read endpoints. Use read-replicas for vector search queries. Optimize Docker build size and node resource allocation in EKS.
contingency_plan: Scale up RDS instance size or offload vector search to a dedicated service (e.g., Pinecone) if RDS CPU saturates.
monitoring_trigger: API Gateway p95 latency alerts exceeding 200ms or RDS CPU > 70%.

risk_description: Vendor lock-in and cost escalation associated with AWS managed services (EKS, OpenSearch, RDS, NAT Gateways) exceeding initial budget estimates.
root_cause: High reliance on managed services for SOC 2 compliance and operational ease.
mitigation_strategy: Implement aggressive auto-scaling policies (scale-to-zero for non-prod). Use Spot Instances for AI worker nodes. Configure OpenSearch index lifecycle policies to archive old logs to S3 Glacier.
contingency_plan: Migrate non-critical workloads to standard EC2 or self-hosted alternatives if costs become unsustainable.
monitoring_trigger: AWS daily spend forecast exceeding budget by > 10%.

risk_description: Development velocity bottleneck due to high complexity of local development environment setup (Kubernetes, AWS mocks, Microservices) for new developers.
root_cause: Heavy dependency chain requiring Docker, K8s, LocalStack, and multiple languages (Node/Python) to run the full stack.
mitigation_strategy: Create a robust `docker-compose` setup that mocks AWS services via LocalStack. Invest in remote development environments (e.g., GitHub Codespaces) with pre-configured containers.
contingency_plan: Simplify local stack to run only core services and stub external dependencies/microservices.
monitoring_trigger: Onboarding time for new developers exceeding 3 days.
